items.title: India Banks Slash Short-Term Debt Sales on Cheaper Forex Funding
items.description: Indian lenders are pulling back short-term debt sales as the central bank’s recent push to attract foreign-currency deposits has opened up a cheaper and more durable source of funding.

items.title: Bankers Say Asia Loan Market to Stay Weak as War Saps Confidence

items.title: Chip Stock Bulls Count on Samsung to Soothe AI Trade Jitters
items.description: A wild ride for global chip stocks in recent weeks has left investors looking for fresh validation of the artificial intelligence trade. Samsung Electronics Co. may provide just that on Tuesday.

items.title: Gold Steadies After Weekly Gain as Rate-Hike Worries Recede
items.description: Gold steadied after posting its first weekly advance since May, supported by reduced expectations that the US Federal Reserve will hike interest rates.

items.title: CATL Invests in New Zealand Firm to Develop Graphite From Wood
items.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is investing in a New Zealand-headquartered company that converts forestry byproducts into graphite for use in lithium batteries.

items.title: Trump’s War Means Higher Global Interest Rates for Years to Come
items.description: Donald Trump’s war against Iran may be over, but the repercussions for global monetary policy are here to stay.

items.title: Gold Miner Genesis Makes Rival $3.9 Billion Bid for Vault
items.description: Australian gold miner Genesis Minerals Ltd. has launched a A$5.6 billion ($3.9 billion) cash and stock bid for Vault Minerals Ltd., trumping a deal agreed earlier with rival Regis Resources Ltd.

items.title: Asian Shares Pare Initial Gains, Dollar Edges Up: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian stocks and US equity-index futures pared their initial gains Monday as technology stocks lost momentum. The dollar strengthened and crude oil trimmed its earlier drop.

items.title: Oil Drops as Hormuz Flows Persist and OPEC+ Flags Higher Supply
items.description: Oil fell as flows through the Strait of Hormuz persisted and OPEC+ signaled higher supplies, fanning concerns about a glut.

items.description: The US bond market faces a test of investor demand for longer-dated maturities, with auctions of 10- and 30-year Treasuries highlighting an otherwise light week for economic events.

items.title: EasyJet Agrees to Castlelake’s Latest Bid Topping £5 Billion
items.description: EasyJet Plc agreed in principle to a takeover offer of more than £5 billion from Castlelake LP, which swooped in as the UK budget carrier was reeling from soaring jet fuel prices and suppressed demand
